title: WordPress REST API — Block Renderer
description: API consumer server-side renders a registered Gutenberg block via the block renderer endpoint

actors.name: API Consumer
actors.description: External client (editor, plugin, theme) that requests server-side block rendering

actors.name: WP_Block_Type_Registry
actors.description: Singleton registry of all registered Gutenberg block types; provides get_registered(name)

use_cases.name: Render Block via REST API
use_cases.description: Request server-side rendering of a registered dynamic Gutenberg block

use_cases.name: Check Block Render Permission
use_cases.description: Verify caller has sufficient capability before rendering. If post_id > 0: requires edit_post on that post. Otherwise: requires edit_posts globally.

use_cases.name: Validate Block Attributes
use_cases.description: Validate and sanitize incoming attributes against the block's registered JSON schema (block->get_attributes()). Uses rest_validate_value_from_schema / rest_sanitize_value_from_schema.

use_cases.name: Execute Block Render
use_cases.description: Look up the registered block, verify it is dynamic (has render_callback), then call render_block() to produce HTML. Optionally sets up global $post context via setup_postdata(post_id).
steps: Build block array: {blockName, attrs, innerHTML:'', innerContent:[]}
render_block($block) — invokes block's render_callback with all filters

use_cases.name: Retrieve Block Output Schema
use_cases.description: Return JSON Schema for the rendered-block response object. Schema defines 'rendered' (string, required, context:edit).

use_cases.name: Register Block Renderer Route
use_cases.description: On REST API init, register the route pattern for GET and POST methods. Performed once at boot.

key_classes.name: WP_REST_Block_Renderer_Controller
methods: get_item_schema(): array — JSON Schema for rendered-block

key_relationships: WP_REST_Block_Renderer_Controller depends_on WP_Block_Type (registered block, is_dynamic(), get_attributes())
business_rules: Only dynamic blocks (those with a render_callback) can be rendered via this endpoint
Block name must match pattern: namespace/block-name (e.g., core/latest-posts)
Requires edit_posts capability at minimum; edit_post on specific post if post_id is provided
Attributes are validated AND sanitized against the block's own registered attribute schema
render_block() is used (not direct callback) to ensure all WordPress filters are applied
Response schema context is 'edit' only — rendered HTML is not exposed in public context
important_notes: Since WordPress 5.0.0. Supports GET (query params) and POST (body). post_id param sets global $post context for blocks that need it (e.g., dynamic blocks querying current post). Route: /wp/v2/block-renderer/{namespace}/{block-name}.
